sections.a_description: Diseases of denial. Building for a market that doesn't exist, pricing for customers who don't care, and calling it a strategy.

a_image.alt: Empty market stall in a dark bazaar with digital price tags floating, market validation concept

sections.a_description: The diseases that kill products after you build them but before they grow.

items.a_description: You talked to five friends who said "that's cool." That's not validation — that's politeness.

a_image.alt: Large magnifying glass hovering over a crowd of small wooden figurines, one figurine glowing under the lens, dark background, dramatic spotlight, shallow depth of field

items.a_description: The market research says there's a gap. But a gap in the market doesn't mean there's a market in the gap.

a_image.alt: Compass spinning wildly on a dark map with question marks, market direction uncertainty metaphor

items.a_description: You have users, but they're not staying. You have revenue, but it's not growing. Something fits — but not quite.

a_image.alt: Puzzle piece floating above a puzzle board almost fitting but not quite, dark atmospheric lighting

items.a_description: Your product is free, freemium, or priced so low it signals "hobby project." Charging real money feels like a betrayal.

a_image.alt: Price tag with the number scratched out multiple times, pricing indecision, dark dramatic lighting

seo.title: Market — Diseases of Denial | Sickness Labs
seo.description: Customer validation, market validation, product-market fit, pricing phobia. The diseases of building for a market that doesn't exist.
